source_paper_title: Analysis of the Primal-Dual Central Path for Nonlinear Semidefinite Optimization Without the Nondegeneracy Condition
context:
Section 4 (Concluding remarks and future work), page 19 (PDF page 19/36)

There exist two directions for future works. The first one is concerned with limiting behavior of the tangential direction \dot{x}(\mu) in the x-space as \mu \to 0. We have the following conjecture: lim_{\mu\to 0} \dot{x}(\mu) = \xi^*, where \xi^* was defined in Proposition 5.
title: Conjectured limit of the central-path tangential direction in NSDP
latex:
Consider the nonlinear semidefinite program (NSDP)
\[
\min_{x\in\mathbb{R}^n} f(x)\quad\text{subject to}\quad G(x)\in \mathrm{S}^m_+,\; h(x)=0,
\]
where \(f:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}\), \(G:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathrm{S}^m\), and \(h:\mathbb{R}^n\to\mathbb{R}^s\) are twice continuously differentiable, \(\mathrm{S}^m\) is the space of real symmetric \(m\times m\) matrices, and \(\mathrm{S}^m_+\) is the cone of positive semidefinite matrices.

Let \(x^*\) be a KKT point and assume:
- strict complementarity holds at \((x^*,Y^a)\), i.e., \(G(x^*)+Y^a\in\mathrm{S}^m_{++}\) for some Lagrange multiplier matrix \(Y^a\succeq 0\) with \(G(x^*)\bullet Y^a=0\),
- the enhanced second-order sufficient condition holds at \(x^*\), and
- the Mangasarian--Fromovitz constraint qualification holds at \(x^*\).

For each \(\mu>0\), let \(w(\mu)=(x(\mu),Y(\mu),z(\mu))\in\mathbb{R}^n\times \mathrm{S}^m\times\mathbb{R}^s\) be the unique smooth primal-dual central path of barrier KKT (BKKT) points satisfying
\[
\nabla f(x(\mu)) - JG(x(\mu))^*Y(\mu) + \nabla h(x(\mu))z(\mu)=0,\quad h(x(\mu))=0,
\]
\[
G(x(\mu))Y(\mu)=\mu I,\quad G(x(\mu))\in\mathrm{S}^m_{++},\; Y(\mu)\in\mathrm{S}^m_{++},
\]
and \(\lim_{\mu\downarrow 0} w(\mu) = (x^*,Y^a,z^a)\), where \((Y^a,z^a)\) is the analytic center of the multiplier set \(\Lambda(x^*)\).

Define \(\xi^*\in\mathbb{R}^n\) to be the unique \(\Delta x\)-component of any solution \((\Delta x,\Delta Y)\) to the linear system (with \(G^*:=G(x^*)\))
\[
U_{x^*}^\top\bigl(\nabla^2_{xx}L(x^*,Y^a,z^a)\,\Delta x - JG(x^*)^*\Delta Y\bigr)=0,
\]
\[
L_{G^*}(\Delta Y) + L_{Y^a}(\Delta G(x^*;\Delta x)) = 2I,
\]
\[
\nabla h(x^*)^\top\Delta x = 0,
\]
where \(L_X(W)=XW+WX\), \(\Delta G(x^*;d)=\sum_{i=1}^n d_i\,\partial G(x^*)/\partial x_i\), and \(U_{x^*}\) has columns forming an orthonormal basis of
\[
U^*:=\{d\in\mathbb{R}^n\mid \Delta G_{EE}(x^*;d)=0,\;\nabla h(x^*)^\top d=0\},
\]
with \(\Delta G_{EE}(x^*;d)=E^{*\top}\Delta G(x^*;d)E^*\) defined via an eigen-decomposition of \(G(x^*)\) as in the paper.

problem (conjecture): Prove (or disprove) that the tangential direction of the primal component of the central path converges to \(\xi^*\), i.e.,
\[
\lim_{\mu\downarrow 0} \dot x(\mu) = \xi^*,
\]
where \(\dot x(\mu)=\frac{d}{d\mu}x(\mu)\) denotes the derivative of the central-path primal variable with respect to the barrier parameter \(\mu\).
location_in_paper: Section 4 (Concluding remarks and future work), page 19 (PDF page 19/36)
